turns.user_message: I noticed lately that I catch myself holding my breath every time a message comes through, waiting to see if the tone feels right. That never used to bother me at all, but now I find myself reading between the lines way too much. A simple short reply sits in my head all afternoon, replaying itself until I convince myself something is off. I just wish I could go back to taking things at face value without feeling this weird knot in my chest every single time.

I keep noticing how this habit has completely taken over my days, turning every quiet moment into a puzzle I feel forced to solve. I track little shifts in how people respond, cataloging every pause or delayed answer like it means something deeper. I used to just let conversations flow naturally without keeping score, but now I am constantly bracing for the other shoe to drop. The mental math is exhausting, and I am tired of feeling like I have to carefully measure my own words just to keep the peace.

I honestly do not recognize the person I have become because this constant scanning has drained every bit of my usual calm. Every single interaction feels like walking across a tightrope where one wrong step ruins everything, and I am so worn out from trying to predict the weather in someone else’s mood. I miss the version of me that could just exist without overthinking every glance or sigh. It feels like I am carrying a heavy weight that keeps growing heavier, and I do not know how to put it down without everything falling apart.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
